# Code of Conduct Enforcement

## Reporting

To report a code of conduct violation, email **conduct@nself.org**. Reports are reviewed by the project founder and treated with confidentiality.

Include: what happened, when, who was involved, any links or evidence. Anonymous reports are accepted but limit our ability to follow up.

## Response Timeline

- **24h:** acknowledgement that your report was received.
- **7-day investigation window:** investigation complete, action taken or explanation given.
- If circumstances require more time, you will receive an update within 7 days explaining the delay.

## Enforcement Ladder

Enforcement is proportional to the severity and pattern of behaviour.

| Action | When used |
|---|---|
| **Warning (1st offence)** | Minor violation. Private message explaining what was wrong and what behaviour is expected. |
| **Temporary ban** | Repeated minor violations or a single serious violation. Temporary removal from community spaces (GitHub issues, Discussions, Discord). Duration specified in the action. |
| **Permanent ban** | Sustained or severe violations. Harassment, hate speech, threats. Permanent removal from all project spaces. |

## Appeal Process

If you believe enforcement action was applied in error, email conduct@nself.org with the subject line "Appeal request — [your GitHub username]" within 14 days of the action. Appeals are reviewed by a secondary contact (see below) to avoid conflict of interest.

## BDFL Conflict Escalation

If a report involves the project founder, the secondary contact handles the investigation independently.

**Secondary contact for BDFL-conflict cases:** nSelf Lead Maintainer (contact details shared privately with the current CODEOWNERS). If no secondary contact has been formally appointed, a neutral third-party mediator from the open source community will be engaged.

## Scope

This enforcement policy applies to:
- GitHub repositories under nself-org
- GitHub Discussions, issues, and pull requests
- Discord server
- Any official nSelf community space

It does not apply to behaviour in unrelated spaces, except where that behaviour creates safety risks for community members.
